title: People Hate Datacenters, Survey Finds
summary: The data drops as Sen. Bernie Sanders calls for a moratorium on datacenter construction. 'We need to take a deep breath. We need to make sure that AI and robotics work for all of us, not just a handful of billionaires.'
reason: Datacenter expansion represents a concrete enclosure of shared infrastructure (land, water, energy grids) for private AI extraction, and the survey reveals distributed popular resistance to that enclosure becoming visible.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool to show how datacenter siting decisions route public commons — water, land, energy, municipal planning capacity — into private AI infrastructure, while the commune layer of local resistance attempts to block or redirect those flows.

title: 'AI Is African Intelligence': The Workers Who Train AI Are Fighting Back

reason: This story directly shows the commune layer forming in response to extraction — workers building an organizational form to route care and solidarity through each other rather than through the platform intermediary — while also exposing the racial and geographic architecture of AI enclosure.
angle: Use 'routing architecture' to show how the Data Labelers Association is attempting to reroute value flows away from platform extraction, and 'enclosure' to name how AI companies depend on a common (distributed human intelligence, cultural knowledge) they cannot produce but systematically underpay.

title: Veterans Who Depend on Mental Health Care Keep Losing Their Therapists Under Trump
summary: The post Veterans Who Depend on Mental Health Care Keep Losing Their Therapists Under Trump appeared first on ProPublica .
reason: The destruction of VA mental health staffing is a concrete case of centralized state disinvestment attacking a care infrastructure that vulnerable people depend on — the commune layer of publicly-provided therapeutic relationships being deliberately dismantled.

title: Amid Crowded Skies, FAA Kills Rule Aimed at Regulating Space Junk
summary: The post Amid Crowded Skies, FAA Kills Rule Aimed at Regulating Space Junk appeared first on ProPublica .
reason: The FAA withdrawing space debris regulation illustrates how regulatory capture operates when private launch companies successfully route public commons (orbital space) into private use without accountability infrastructure.
angle: Use the 'alibi structure' tool: the alibi that self-regulation or market incentives will manage orbital commons is becoming visible as an alibi precisely as the commons degrades — the FAA's retreat is the alibi dropping its mask.

title: Mantengan a ICE fuera de las tiendas, dicen los trabajadores de Starbucks
summary: Desde que más de 4.000 agentes del ICE llegaron a la ciudad de Minneapolis, el barista de Starbucks Alex Rivers ha intentado a equilibrar la rigurosa concentración que exige el trabajo–se espera que los baristas escriban en cada vaso y completen cada pedido en cuatro minutos o menos, según él–con el miedo persistente de que los agentes puedan irrumpir en cualquier momento.

title: Welcome, Paul and Lee! Farewell, Barbara
summary: Labor Notes is delighted to welcome two terrific new staff writer-organizers and three great interns as we get ready for our big conference in Chicago in June. We’re also bidding a fond farewell to a retiring colleague. Paul Prescod , staff writer/organizer, started in January. He has been a public school teacher and rank-and-file activist in the Philadelphia Federation of Teachers, and a staff organizer with Teamsters for a Democratic Union. He covers transit workers, K-12 education, and the building trades in the clean energy economy.
reason: This is an internal staffing announcement with no analytical content relevant to the framework.

title: Strike Mobilization Leads to Reformer Win in Oregon Nurses
summary: The 24,000-member Oregon Nurses Association has elected a slate of reform candidates to statewide leadership positions. The Caucus for Powerful Reform (CPR) won 15 out of the 21 seats it ran for, including the presidency, vice-presidency, secretary, and a majority of board of director seats.
reason: A rank-and-file reform caucus winning union leadership through strike mobilization is a concrete example of flow-register organizing — changing the routing architecture of labor power rather than just swapping messaging.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool: CPR didn't just run new faces, they built a mobilization circuit through strike action that rewired how power flows inside the union, making this a structural rather than representational change.

title: “We Don’t Want To Be Studied Like Guinea Pigs. We Want To Be Helped.”
summary: Three years after the East Palestine derailment, residents are still suffering.
reason: The story shows the epistemic incompetence of centralization in operation: state and corporate actors systematically replace actual remediation with data collection, converting suffering people into research objects while the commune layer of mutual aid and collective knowledge-sharing remains the primary support structure.
angle: Use 'epistemic incompetence of centralization' to show how regulatory capture converts a care obligation into a knowledge-extraction operation — studying rather than helping is not negligence but the structural logic of systems that need the problem to remain legible as data rather than resolved as harm.
